hypothesis: If adaptive diversity constraints informed by NaviDiv’s multi-level metrics are applied during reinforcement learning for singlet-fission molecule generation, then the resulting libraries will show significantly higher chemical diversity (e.g., lower mean Tanimoto similarity, more clusters, higher unique fragment/scaffold percentages) without decreasing the hit rate for the ΔE_SF criterion, because dynamic penalties on overrepresented structures mitigate mode collapse while preserving optimization of the target property.
